rulings.Case Title: INVESTMENT RETRIEVERS, INC. A CALIFORNIA VS. JORGE CABALLERO AKA JORGE DE JESUS CABALLERO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default And Default Judgment For Lack Iof Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 26, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT JORGE CABALLERO AKA JORGE DE JESUS CABALLERO'S Ntc Of Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default And Default Judgment For Lack of Ntc Granted. Defendant lacked actual notice which was not caused by avoidance or inexcusable neglect. The Contreras declaration submitted by Defendant does not prove otherwise. Defendant timely filed the instant motion upon discovering the litigation. Defendant has met the requirements to set aside a default under Code of Civil Procedure section 473.5. Further, it appears that a request for entry of default was made prematurely on May 2, 2011. Even if the complaint had been served on April 8, 2011 by substituted service and mail, it was not completed until April 18, 2011. (Code of Civil Procedure section 415.20.) Thus, the response to the complaint was not due until thirty days had elapsed on May 20, 2011. (Code of Civil Procedure section 412.20(a)(3).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 26, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANTs IRENE CHEUNG & TIEMPO INTERIORS, INC.'s (Corrected) Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Served On Cho Kwan Cpa, Inc. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MENDALA VS. IRENE CHEUNG, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoena For Productionof Business Records Served On Cho Kwan,Cpa,Inc. And Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 26, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, DEFENDANTs IRENE CHEUNG & TIEMPO INTERIORS, INC.'s Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoena For Production of Business Records Served On Cho Kwan, Cpa, Inc. And Motion For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to quash or modify the subpoena for business records is denied. The corrected notice to quash or modify the deposition subpoena is also denied. No sanctions are awarded to any party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NATALIA KRUEGER VS. URBAN MAPPING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Striking Pltf'S First Amended Complaint; Prayer No. 3
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 26, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANTS URBAN MAPPING, INC., IAN WHITE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Striking Pltf'S First Amended Complaint; Prayer No. 3 Denied. Plaintiff has pled a statutory basis for attorney fees pursuant to Labor Code § 218.5. Plaintiff alleges that Defendants failed to pay her wages she earned as an employee, including PTO and other benefits provided to other employees. (See FAC, 27 - 29.) To the extent that Defendants attack the sufficiency of the allegations of the fourth cause of action, this is not the proper subject of a motion to strike. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. As the Court previously ruled, the Second Amended Complaint states a cause of action for violation of the CLRA and that claim supports a claim for punitive damages. rulings.Case Title: ADRIANA URIBE VS. SUTTER HEALTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 26, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress is overruled. See Ochoa v. Super. Ct. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 159. The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for violation of the UCL is overruled. Plaintiff can obtain restitution and possibly an injunction if the misconduct is continuing. See In re Tobacco II Cases, 46 Cal.4th 298, 321. In making this ruling, the Court has considered only the allegations in the pleadings and not any facts set forth solely in plaintiff's memorandum of points and authorities. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SAVEMUNI.COM, AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION OF et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO RECREATION AND PARK COMMISSION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 26, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO RECREATION AND PARK COMMISSION, MAYOR EDWIN M. LEE IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS, BOARD OF SUPERVISORS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, RPD DIRECTOR OF OPERATIONS DENNIS KERN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Plaintiffs' unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted except as to Exhibit L as to which there is a dispute about its authenticity. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend pursuant Civil Procedure Code section 430.10(e) for failure to state facts necessary to constitute a cause of action under applicable law. The proposed subway station is not a structure for "non-recreational purposes" within the meaning of Section 4.113(2) of the San Francisco City Charter. The proposed subway station at Union Square Park incidentally promotes public access to the park, and is a recreational purpose consistent with park purposes and public enjoyment of the park. Humphreys v. City and County of San Francisco (1928) 92 Cal. App. 2d 69. Citizens Planning Assn. v. City of Santa Barbara (2011) 191 Cal. App. 4th 1541, upon which Plaintiffs rely, is distinguishable. That case turned on the fact that the proposed use of park land was to provide access to a private residential development, which is not the case here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
